schema_version: v0 — migrates when Q6 (intention envelope) lands
owner: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC) — CC gates writes; the systemic-learning intent is Akien's ratified I-learns-its-gates.
intention: Make 'how does this component learn?' a contract question asked of every component — a common process where only the endpoints change (what the component is, what its gates are, what counts as evidence). The learning-injection is a pattern, not a per-component reinvention; enforced eventually by physics (schema/tester), interim by prose IOU.
why: Gate-learning must be systemic or it dies as scattered one-offs. The same move that forced the why into intention+why.json (CP3 as schema, not a fillable field) applies to learning: the component contract should force a learning-answer. It will not fit every component — but we ask how it fits before discarding it, never assume it doesn't. A pattern earns its abstraction from instances, so this crystallizes once cc-learning-store + a second endpoint share the process with only their endpoints differing.
gates: proof gate (tester) — IOU: tester not built AND the enforcement itself is future-physics (schema field / tester check that a component's charter answers 'how does it learn?'). Interim: a prose IOU in CLAUDE.md 'Rules awaiting physics' + applied by hand to each new component's charter. Durability gate (code-seam): not done until tested AND committed.
falsifier: a second component (endpoint) plugs into the SAME learning process, changing only its endpoints — the shared middle did not have to be rewritten. Wrong-intent signal: if every component needs a bespoke learning mechanism (no shared middle survives), it is not a pattern — retract the abstraction and stop forcing the question.
horizon: re-check when the second endpoint arrives (does the shared process hold?); and when the tester exists (the prose IOU retires into a schema/tester check, shrinking CLAUDE.md's rules-awaiting-physics list).
touches: CLAUDE.md 'Rules awaiting physics' — add the interim IOU: every component's charter answers 'how does this component learn?' → schema/tester enforcement pending. (Propose before editing; CLAUDE.md stands the /challenge gate.)
the component/charter convention — extend intention+why.json expectations with the learning-answer.
extract the shared process once cc-learning-store + a 2nd endpoint exist (don't abstract from n=1).
depends_on: cc-learning-store (the first endpoint) — the pattern generalizes FROM instances, so it does not close before a second endpoint exists.
provenance: cast 2026-07-16 via /sorted, under I-learns-its-gates. Names the standing practice from the ratified amendment: ask how every component learns, before discarding it.
